roots.title: Course Logistics and Pandas Data Types
roots.summary: Students should access code for Data 100 on the course website, which includes resources for lecture two on pandas, notebooks, and data files. An HTML version of the code is available for mobile use but is not runnable. A translation of data science libraries to pandas is provided, along with a data folder in the notebooks containing necessary data. In pandas, integer values are recognized as 64-bit integers, while non-primitive types like strings or custom objects are categorized as objects, which may cause issues if numerical values are loaded as objects, requiring a type cast back to the correct format.

children.title: Course Logistics and Code Access for Data 100
children.summary: Students should familiarize themselves with the code available on the course website for Data 100, which includes relevant code for lecture two on pandas, notebooks, and data files. An HTML version of the code is accessible for mobile use, though it is not runnable. Additionally, a translation of data science libraries to pandas is provided, along with a data folder in the notebooks containing necessary data and resources.

children.title: Course Logistics and Code Access for Data 100
children.summary: Students should familiarize themselves with the code available on the course website for Data 100. This includes all relevant code for lecture two on pandas, notebooks, and data files. An HTML version of the code is available for mobile access, though it is not runnable. Additionally, a translation of data science libraries to pandas is provided, along with a data folder in the notebooks containing necessary data and resources.

children.title: Course Logistics and Code Access for Data 100
children.summary: Students are expected to familiarize themselves with the code provided in the course, which will be available on the course website. The website will host all relevant code for lecture two on pandas, including notebooks and data files. An HTML version of the code is also available for mobile access, although it is not runnable. Additionally, there is a translation of data science libraries to pandas included, along with a data folder in the notebooks containing all necessary data and resources.

There's also the HTML version if you're looking on your phone and you just want to take a look at the code you can open the HTML version that is not runable. It's just the HTML file that you should be able to take a look. And this is kind of an unofficial translation of data science library to pendants. So it basically walks you through everything that you had and then it will have the corresponding version in pandas. children.title: Pandas Data Types and Object Handling
children.summary: In pandas, integer values are recognized as 64-bit integers, while non-primitive types such as strings or custom objects are categorized as objects. This distinction can lead to issues if numerical values are loaded as objects, necessitating a type cast back to the correct format.

children.title: Pandas Data Types and Object Handling
children.summary: In pandas, integer values are automatically recognized as 64-bit integers, while non-primitive types like strings or custom objects are categorized as objects. This distinction can cause problems if numerical values are improperly loaded as objects, requiring a type cast back to the correct format.

children.title: Pandas Data Types and Object Handling
children.summary: In pandas, integer values are automatically labeled as 64-bit integers, but non-primitive types such as strings or custom objects are tagged as objects. This can lead to issues where numerical values may be incorrectly loaded as objects if the data isn't loaded properly, necessitating a cast back to the appropriate type.

leaf_chunk.text:
Audio: Any questions so far? Questions? Great question. So I forgot to mention that here because my values were integer, it was automatically labeling that as in 64 and it's not integers in 64 bit. And that's how pandas is implemented. But anything other than primitive types, integer float, everything else is going to be tagged as objects and you'll keep seeing object object over and over again. So most of the time, it is not numerical value and it's objects. And a lot of time, if you, for example, don't do the loading of the data properly, it will even load the integer values or full load values as object, then you need to cast that. Video: 1: v
